title: Introduction
text:
This assignment will test how well you’re able to perform various data science-related tasks.
Each Problem Group below will center around a particular dataset that you have worked with before.
To ensure you receive full credit for a question, make sure you demonstrate the appropriate polars, lets-plot, or other commands as requested in the provided code blocks.
You may find that some questions require multiple steps to fully answer. Others require some mental arithmetic in addition to polars commands. Use your best judgment.

title: Introduction
text:
Submission
Each problem group asks a series of questions. This assignment consists of two submissions:

After completing the questions below, open the Module 01 Assessment Quiz in Canvas and enter your answers to these questions there.
After completing and submitting the quiz, save this Colab notebook as a GitHub Gist (You’ll need to create a GitHub account for this), by selecting Save a copy as a GitHub Gist from the File menu above.
In Canvas, open the Module 01 Assessment GitHub Gist assignment and paste the GitHub Gist URL for this notebook. Then submit that assignment.

title: Introduction
text:
Problem Group 1
For the questions in this group, you’ll work with the Netflix Movies Dataset found at this url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byui-cse/cse450-course/master/data/netflix_titles.csv

Question 1
Load the dataset into a Polars data frame and determine what data type is used to store the release_year feature.

import polars as pl
url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byui-cse/cse450-course/master/data/netflix_titles.csv"
df = pl.read_csv(url)
print(df['release_year'].dtype)

Int64




Question 2
Filter your dataset so it contains only TV Shows. How many of those TV Shows were rated TV-Y7?

df_T = df.filter((pl.col("type") == "TV Show") & (pl.col("rating") == "TV-Y7"))
df_T.height

100




Question 3
Further filter your dataset so it only contains TV Shows released between the years 2000 and 2009 inclusive. How many of those shows were rated TV-Y7?

df_test = df.filter((pl.col("type") == "TV Show") & (pl.col("release_year") <= 2009) & (pl.col("release_year") >= 2000 ) & (pl.col("rating") == "TV-Y7"))
df_test

title: Introduction
text:
Problem Group 2
For the questions in this group, you’ll work with the Cereal Dataset found at this url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byui-cse/cse450-course/master/data/cereal.csv

Question 4
After importing the dataset into a polars data frame, determine the median amount of protein in cereal brands manufactured by Kelloggs. (mfr code “K”)

import polars as pl

url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byui-cse/cse450-course/master/data/cereal.csv"
df = pl.read_csv(url)
test=df.filter(mfr="K")
test['protein'].median()

Question 5
In order to comply with new government regulations, all cereals must now come with a “Healthiness” rating. This rating is calculated based on this formula:
healthiness = (protein + fiber) / sugar
Create a new healthiness column populated with values based on the above formula.
Then, determine the median healthiness value for only General Mills cereals (mfr = “G”), rounded to two decimal places.

t = df.filter(mfr="G")
t = t.with_columns(healthiness = (pl.col('protein') + pl.col('fiber'))/pl.col('sugars'))
t['healthiness'].median()

0.475

title: Introduction
text:
Problem Group 3
For the questions in this group, you’ll work with the Titanic Dataset found at this url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byui-cse/cse450-course/master/data/titanic.csv

Question 6
After loading the dataset into a polars DataFrame, create a new column called NameGroup that contains the first letter of the passenger’s surname in lower case.
Note that in the dataset, passenger’s names are provided in the Name column and are listed as:
Surname, Given names
For example, if a passenger’s Name is Braund, Mr. Owen Harris, the NameGroup column should contain the value b.
Then count how many passengers have a NameGroup value of k.

import polars as pl
url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byui-cse/cse450-course/master/data/titanic.csv"
df = pl.read_csv(url)
df_titanic = df.with_columns(NameGroup = pl.col('Name').str.slice(0, 1).str.to_lowercase())
k_count = df_titanic.filter(NameGroup="k").height
k_count

28
